name: Physics Fundamentals
description: Introductory physics covering mechanics, forces, energy, waves, electricity, magnetism, and modern physics — developing problem-solving skills through real-world applications and conceptual understanding.

modules.title: Measurement & Scientific Notation
modules.objectives: Use SI units and dimensional analysis; apply scientific notation and significant figures; distinguish vectors from scalars.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Physics 2e — Ch 1: The Nature of Science and Physics
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy Physics — Introduction to Vectors and Scalars
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Physics #3 — Vectors and 2D Motion
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Significant Figures and Scientific Notation practice
modules.assignment: Convert 5 quantities between SI unit prefixes (e.g., km to m, µg to kg). Write 4 numbers in scientific notation and identify the number of significant figures in each.

modules.title: Kinematics: Motion in 1D
modules.objectives: Define displacement, velocity, and acceleration; apply kinematic equations to solve 1D problems including free fall.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Physics 2e — Ch 2: Kinematics in One Dimension
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Physics #1 — Motion in a Straight Line
modules.assignment: A car accelerates from rest at 3 m/s² for 8 seconds. Calculate its final velocity and distance traveled. Then solve a free-fall problem: how long does a ball take to fall 45 m from rest?

modules.title: Kinematics: Motion in 2D
modules.objectives: Resolve vectors into components; analyze projectile motion and relative motion in two dimensions.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Physics 2e — Ch 3: Motion in Two Dimensions
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Physics #3 — Vectors and 2D Motion
modules.assignment: A ball is launched horizontally at 15 m/s from a height of 20 m. Calculate (a) time of flight, (b) horizontal range, and (c) velocity at impact. Show vector components at each step.

modules.title: Newton's Laws of Motion
modules.objectives: State and apply Newton's three laws; draw free body diagrams; use F = ma to solve net force problems.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Physics 2e — Ch 4: Dynamics: Force and Newton's Laws
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy Physics — Newton's Laws of Motion
modules.assignment: Draw a free body diagram for a 10 kg box being pushed across a frictionless surface with 50 N. Calculate its acceleration. Then describe a real-world example of Newton's third law with action and reaction pair labeled.

modules.title: Forces: Friction, Tension & Gravity
modules.objectives: Calculate normal force, static and kinetic friction, tension in strings, and gravitational force; distinguish weight from mass.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Physics 2e — Ch 5: Further Applications of Newton's Laws
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy Physics — Inclined Planes and Friction
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Friction and Inclined Planes practice
modules.assignment: A 5 kg block is on a surface with µk = 0.3. A horizontal force of 20 N is applied. Calculate (a) normal force, (b) kinetic friction force, and (c) net acceleration. Also find the weight of the block in newtons.

modules.title: Work, Energy & Power
modules.objectives: Apply the work-energy theorem; calculate kinetic and potential energy; use conservation of mechanical energy; define power.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Physics 2e — Ch 7: Work, Energy, and Energy Resources
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy Physics — Introduction to Work and Energy
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Physics #9 — Work, Energy, and Power
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Work and Kinetic Energy practice
modules.assignment: A 2 kg ball is dropped from a height of 10 m. Use conservation of energy to find its speed just before hitting the ground. Then calculate the power output of an engine that does 6000 J of work in 30 seconds.

modules.title: Momentum & Impulse
modules.objectives: Calculate linear momentum and impulse; apply conservation of momentum to elastic and inelastic collisions.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Physics 2e — Ch 8: Linear Momentum and Collisions
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy Physics — Introduction to Momentum
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Physics #10 — Momentum and Impulse
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Conservation of Momentum practice
modules.assignment: A 3 kg cart moving at 4 m/s collides with a stationary 1 kg cart. If they stick together (perfectly inelastic), find their common velocity. Then solve for the impulse exerted when a 0.5 kg ball changes velocity from −10 m/s to +10 m/s.

modules.title: Circular Motion & Gravitation
modules.objectives: Calculate centripetal acceleration and force; apply Newton's law of universal gravitation; describe orbital motion.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Physics 2e — Ch 6: Uniform Circular Motion and Gravitation
modules.assignment: A 1000 kg car travels around a circular track of radius 50 m at 20 m/s. Calculate the centripetal force. Then use the universal gravitation formula to find the gravitational force between Earth (6×10²⁴ kg) and a 70 kg person on the surface (r = 6.4×10⁶ m).

modules.title: Waves & Sound
modules.objectives: Identify wave properties (amplitude, wavelength, frequency, speed); explain reflection, refraction, and interference; analyze sound intensity and the Doppler effect.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Physics 2e — Ch 16: Oscillatory Motion and Waves
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy Physics — Introduction to Waves
modules.assignment: A wave has a frequency of 440 Hz and a wavelength of 0.77 m. Calculate its speed. Explain using a diagram how destructive interference occurs between two identical waves. Describe the Doppler effect with one everyday example.

modules.title: Light & Optics
modules.objectives: Describe the electromagnetic spectrum; apply the law of reflection and Snell's law; analyze images formed by lenses and mirrors.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy Physics — Reflection and Refraction
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Reflection and Refraction practice
modules.assignment: A light ray travels from water (n = 1.33) into air (n = 1.00) at an incident angle of 30°. Use Snell's law to find the refraction angle. Then draw a ray diagram for a converging lens with an object placed outside the focal point.

modules.title: Electrostatics
modules.objectives: Explain electric charge and Coulomb's law; describe electric fields and calculate electric potential difference.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Physics 2e — Ch 18: Electric Charge and Electric Field
modules.assignment: Two point charges of +3 µC and −3 µC are separated by 0.1 m. Calculate the Coulomb force between them (k = 9×10⁹ N·m²/C²). Sketch the resulting electric field pattern around both charges.

modules.title: Electric Circuits
modules.objectives: Apply Ohm's law; calculate current, voltage, and resistance in series and parallel circuits; determine electrical power.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Physics 2e — Ch 21: Circuits and DC Instruments
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy Physics — Electric Circuits and Resistance
modules.assignment: Three resistors (4 Ω, 6 Ω, 12 Ω) are connected in parallel across a 12 V battery. Find the equivalent resistance, total current drawn, and power dissipated by each resistor.

modules.title: Magnetism & Electromagnetism
modules.objectives: Describe magnetic fields and forces on moving charges and current-carrying conductors; explain electromagnetic induction and Faraday's law.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Physics 2e — Ch 22: Magnetism and Ch 23: Electromagnetic Induction
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy Physics — Magnetic Force on Current-Carrying Wire
modules.assignment: A proton (charge 1.6×10⁻¹⁹ C) moves at 3×10⁵ m/s perpendicular to a 0.5 T magnetic field. Calculate the magnetic force on it. Then explain in your own words how a generator uses electromagnetic induction to produce electricity.

modules.title: Modern Physics Introduction
modules.objectives: Describe key ideas of special relativity; explain the photoelectric effect and photons; outline atomic models and basic nuclear physics concepts.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Physics 2e — Ch 28: Special Relativity and Ch 29: Quantum Physics
modules.video2Note: MinutePhysics — Special Relativity and the Photoelectric Effect
modules.assignment: Explain Einstein's two postulates of special relativity in your own words. A photon has a frequency of 6×10¹⁴ Hz. Calculate its energy (E = hf, h = 6.63×10⁻³⁴ J·s). Describe how nuclear fission releases energy using E = mc².

questions.question: Which SI unit is used to measure force?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Newton.

questions.question: A car travels 100 m in 5 s starting from rest with constant acceleration. What is its acceleration?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: 8 m/s².

questions.question: In projectile motion, which component of velocity remains constant (ignoring air resistance)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Horizontal component.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Newton's second law states that net force equals:" is: mass × acceleration.
questions.explanation: True. mass × acceleration is correct.

questions.question: A 10 kg object rests on a surface with µk = 0.4. What is the kinetic friction force?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: 39.2 N.

questions.question: The work done by a force of 20 N acting over 5 m is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: 100 J.

questions.question: Conservation of mechanical energy applies when:
options: A net external force is present
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Only conservative forces act.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "The momentum of a 5 kg object moving at 6 m/s is:" is: 1.2 kg·m/s.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: 30 kg·m/s.

questions.question: In a perfectly inelastic collision, what is conserved?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Momentum only.

questions.question: Which of the following is a scalar quantity?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Speed.

questions.question: An object in free fall (no air resistance) near Earth's surface accelerates at approximately:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: 9.8 m/s² downward.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Power is defined as:" is: Work divided by time.
questions.explanation: True. Work divided by time is correct.

questions.question: A 4 kg object is pushed with a net force of 12 N. (a) Calculate its acceleration. (b) If it starts from rest, how far does it travel in 3 seconds? Show all work.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: (a) a = F/m = 12/4 = 3 m/s². (b) d = ½at² = ½(3)(9) = 13.5 m..

questions.question: Explain the difference between mass and weight. A 60 kg person stands on the Moon where g = 1.6 m/s². Calculate their weight on the Moon.
questions.answer: Mass is the amount of matter (constant everywhere); weight is the gravitational force (W = mg, varies by location). Moon weight = 60 × 1.6 = 96 N.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Mass is the amount of matter (constant everywhere); weight is the gravitational force (W = mg, varies by location). Moon weight = 60 × 1.6 = 96 N..

questions.question: A 2 kg ball drops from a height of 20 m. Using conservation of energy, find its speed just before it hits the ground. (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: PE = mgh = 2 × 10 × 20 = 400 J. At ground: KE = 400 J = ½mv² → v = √(2×400/2) = √400 = 20 m/s..

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Centripetal acceleration always points:" is: Tangent to the circle.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Toward the center of the circle.

questions.question: According to Newton's law of universal gravitation, if the distance between two masses doubles, the gravitational force:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Decreases by four.

questions.question: The speed of a wave is related to its frequency and wavelength by:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: v = f × λ.

questions.question: The Doppler effect explains why a siren sounds higher-pitched when:
options: The source moves away from you
You move away from the source
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The source moves toward you.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Snell's law relates the angle of incidence to the angle of refraction through:" is: n₁ sin θ₁ = n₂ sin θ₂.
questions.explanation: True. n₁ sin θ₁ = n₂ sin θ₂ is correct.

questions.question: Two objects with opposite electric charges will:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Attract each other.

questions.question: Ohm's law states that current through a conductor is:
options: Directly proportional to voltage and inversely to resistance
questions.answer: Directly proportional to voltage and inversely to resistance
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Directly proportional to voltage and inversely to resistance.

questions.question: In a series circuit, the total resistance equals:
options: The sum of all individual resistances
questions.answer: The sum of all individual resistances
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The sum of all individual resistances.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "A magnetic field exerts a force on a charge only when the charge is:" is: Stationary in the field.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Moving relative to the field.

questions.question: Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction states that an EMF is induced when:
options: The magnetic flux through a circuit changes
Voltage is applied to a coil
questions.answer: The magnetic flux through a circuit changes
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The magnetic flux through a circuit changes.

questions.question: Einstein's photoelectric effect demonstrated that light behaves as:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Packets of energy called photons.

questions.question: Special relativity predicts that as an object approaches the speed of light, its mass:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Increases toward infinity.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Nuclear fission releases energy because:" is: Nuclei are split and the products have less mass than the original.
questions.explanation: True. Nuclei are split and the products have less mass than the original is correct.

questions.question: The electromagnetic spectrum from lowest to highest frequency is:
options: Gamma, X-ray, UV, Visible, Infrared, Microwave, Radio
Radio, Microwave, Infrared, Visible, UV, X-ray, Gamma
Visible, Radio, X-ray, Gamma, UV, Microwave, Infrared
UV, Visible, Infrared, Radio, Microwave, X-ray, Gamma
questions.answer: Radio, Microwave, Infrared, Visible, UV, X-ray, Gamma
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Radio, Microwave, Infrared, Visible, UV, X-ray, Gamma.

questions.question: In a parallel circuit, the voltage across each branch is:
options: Equal to the source voltage for each branch
Greater in branches with more resistance
questions.answer: Equal to the source voltage for each branch
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Equal to the source voltage for each branch.

questions.question: Total internal reflection occurs when:
options: Light moves from a less dense to a more dense medium
The incident angle exceeds the critical angle in a denser medium
Light travels parallel to the surface
Wavelength is shorter than the medium thickness
questions.answer: The incident angle exceeds the critical angle in a denser medium
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The incident angle exceeds the critical angle in a denser medium.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Coulomb's law states that the electrostatic force between two charges is:" is: Proportional to the distance between them.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Proportional to the product of charges and inversely proportional to the square of distance.

questions.question: A 1200 kg car moves at 30 m/s and brakes to a stop over 60 m. (a) Calculate the kinetic energy before braking. (b) Find the braking force exerted. Show all work.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: (a) KE = ½mv² = ½(1200)(900) = 540,000 J. (b) Work-energy: F × d = KE → F = 540,000 / 60 = 9,000 N..

questions.question: A circuit has a 9 V battery and two resistors: 3 Ω in series with 6 Ω. Calculate (a) total resistance, (b) current in the circuit, and (c) voltage drop across the 3 Ω resistor.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: (a) R_total = 3 + 6 = 9 Ω. (b) I = V/R = 9/9 = 1 A. (c) V₃ = I × 3 = 1 × 3 = 3 V..

questions.question: Describe the key difference between an elastic and an inelastic collision. Give one real-world example of each type and state which physical quantity is conserved in both cases.
questions.answer: In elastic collisions, both momentum and kinetic energy are conserved (e.g., billiard balls). In inelastic collisions, momentum is conserved but kinetic energy is not — some is lost to heat/deformation (e.g., two clay balls sticking together). Momentum is conserved in both.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: In elastic collisions, both momentum and kinetic energy are conserved (e.g., billiard balls). In inelastic collisions, momentum is conserved but kinetic energy is not — some is lost to heat/deformation (e.g., two clay balls sticking together). Momentum is conserved in both..

quizQuestions.question: How many significant figures are in the number 0.00450?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: 3.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is a vector quantity?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Displacement.

quizQuestions.question: The SI unit of length is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Meter.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "How many significant figures are in the number 0.00450" is: 3.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. 3 is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Displacement differs from distance in that displacement:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Has both magnitude and direction.

quizQuestions.question: An object with zero acceleration is:
options: Moving at constant velocity or at rest
quizQuestions.answer: Moving at constant velocity or at rest
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Moving at constant velocity or at rest.

quizQuestions.question: A ball is thrown straight up. At its highest point its velocity is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Zero.

quizQuestions.question: The kinematic equation v² = v₀² + 2aΔx is most useful when:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Time is unknown.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Displacement differs from distance in that displacement:" is: Has both magnitude and direction.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Has both magnitude and direction is correct.

quizQuestions.question: In projectile motion, the vertical acceleration is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: 9.8 m/s² downward.

quizQuestions.question: A ball launched horizontally will hit the ground at the same time as a ball:
options: Thrown upward at the same speed
Dropped vertically from the same height
quizQuestions.answer: Dropped vertically from the same height
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Dropped vertically from the same height.

quizQuestions.question: To find the x-component of a vector at angle θ with the horizontal, you use:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: v cos θ.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "In projectile motion, the vertical acceleration is:" is: 9.8 m/s² downward.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. 9.8 m/s² downward is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "A ball launched horizontally will hit the ground at the same time as a ball:" is: Thrown upward at the same speed.
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Dropped vertically from the same height.

quizQuestions.question: Newton's first law is also known as the law of:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Inertia.

quizQuestions.question: If a 4 kg object experiences a net force of 16 N, its acceleration is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: 4 m/s².

quizQuestions.question: Newton's third law states that action and reaction forces are:
options: Acting on the same object in opposite directions
Equal in magnitude and opposite in direction, on different objects
Equal only when the system is at rest
quizQuestions.answer: Equal in magnitude and opposite in direction, on different objects
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Equal in magnitude and opposite in direction, on different objects.

quizQuestions.question: A free body diagram shows:
options: All forces acting on an object represented as arrows
The internal structure of an object
quizQuestions.answer: All forces acting on an object represented as arrows
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: All forces acting on an object represented as arrows.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Newton's first law is also known as the law of:" is: Inertia.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Inertia is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The normal force on an object on a flat surface equals:
options: The object's weight (if no vertical acceleration)
quizQuestions.answer: The object's weight (if no vertical acceleration)
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: The object's weight (if no vertical acceleration).

quizQuestions.question: Static friction is always _____ kinetic friction:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Greater than or equal to.

quizQuestions.question: The gravitational force between two masses depends on:
options: Their masses and the distance between them
quizQuestions.answer: Their masses and the distance between them
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Their masses and the distance between them.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The normal force on an object on a flat surface equals:" is: The object's weight (if no vertical acceleration).
quizQuestions.explanation: True. The object's weight (if no vertical acceleration) is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Kinetic energy depends on:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Mass and velocity squared.

quizQuestions.question: Gravitational potential energy is given by:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: mgh.

quizQuestions.question: When a spring is compressed, it stores:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Elastic potential energy.

quizQuestions.question: Work is defined as:
options: Force times distance (in the direction of force)
quizQuestions.answer: Force times distance (in the direction of force)
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Force times distance (in the direction of force).

quizQuestions.question: A 1000 W engine running for 10 seconds does:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: 10,000 J of work.

quizQuestions.question: Momentum is defined as:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Mass times velocity.

quizQuestions.question: Impulse equals:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Force times time.

quizQuestions.question: In an elastic collision:
options: Both momentum and kinetic energy are conserved
quizQuestions.answer: Both momentum and kinetic energy are conserved
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Both momentum and kinetic energy are conserved.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Momentum is defined as:" is: Mass times velocity.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Mass times velocity is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Centripetal force is directed:
options: Toward the center of the circle
quizQuestions.answer: Toward the center of the circle
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Toward the center of the circle.

quizQuestions.question: If the speed of an object in circular motion doubles while radius stays the same, centripetal acceleration:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Quadruples.

quizQuestions.question: Newton's law of universal gravitation states that gravitational force is proportional to:
options: The sum of the masses divided by distance
The product of the masses divided by the square of the distance
quizQuestions.answer: The product of the masses divided by the square of the distance
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: The product of the masses divided by the square of the distance.

quizQuestions.question: A satellite in orbit is in a state of:
options: Weightlessness because it is in free fall toward Earth
Balanced gravitational forces from all sides
quizQuestions.answer: Weightlessness because it is in free fall toward Earth
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Weightlessness because it is in free fall toward Earth.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Centripetal force is directed:" is: Toward the center of the circle.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Toward the center of the circle is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The distance between two consecutive wave crests is called:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Wavelength.

quizQuestions.question: The frequency of a wave is measured in:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Hertz.

quizQuestions.question: Sound waves are classified as:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Longitudinal (compression) waves.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The distance between two consecutive wave crests is called:" is: Wavelength.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Wavelength is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The frequency of a wave is measured in:" is: Meters.
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Hertz.

quizQuestions.question: The law of reflection states that the angle of incidence:
options: Is always greater than the angle of reflection
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Equals the angle of reflection.

quizQuestions.question: When light moves from air into glass it:
options: Speeds up and bends away from normal
Slows down and bends toward the normal
quizQuestions.answer: Slows down and bends toward the normal
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Slows down and bends toward the normal.

quizQuestions.question: Which part of the electromagnetic spectrum has the shortest wavelength?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Gamma rays.

quizQuestions.question: A converging lens produces a real image when the object is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Beyond the focal length.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The law of reflection states that the angle of incidence:" is: Equals the angle of reflection.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Equals the angle of reflection is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Like charges:
options: Have no effect on each other
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Repel each other.

quizQuestions.question: Coulomb's law gives the force between two point charges. If one charge doubles, the force:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Doubles.

quizQuestions.question: An electric field line represents:
options: The direction of force on a positive test charge
quizQuestions.answer: The direction of force on a positive test charge
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: The direction of force on a positive test charge.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Like charges:" is: Repel each other.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Repel each other is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "a conductor in electrostatic equilibrium has the electric field inside equal to:."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Zero.

quizQuestions.question: In Ohm's law, V = IR. If resistance doubles and voltage stays constant, current:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Halves.

quizQuestions.question: In a series circuit, the current through each component is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: The same throughout.

quizQuestions.question: Electrical power dissipated by a resistor equals:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: I² × R.

quizQuestions.question: In a parallel circuit, adding more resistors in parallel:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Decreases total resistance.

quizQuestions.question: The unit of electrical resistance is the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Ohm.

quizQuestions.question: Magnetic field lines exit from which pole of a magnet?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: North pole.

quizQuestions.question: The right-hand rule for a current-carrying wire gives the direction of:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: The surrounding magnetic field.

quizQuestions.question: An electric motor converts:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Electrical energy to mechanical energy.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Magnetic field lines exit from which pole of a magnet" is: North pole.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. North pole is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "a transformer works on the principle of:."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Electromagnetic induction.

quizQuestions.question: According to special relativity, the speed of light in a vacuum is:
options: Constant for all observers regardless of motion
Dependent on the light source's speed
quizQuestions.answer: Constant for all observers regardless of motion
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Constant for all observers regardless of motion.

quizQuestions.question: The photoelectric effect showed that light energy is quantized into packets called:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Photons.

quizQuestions.question: In Rutherford's atomic model, most of an atom's mass is concentrated in:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: A dense central nucleus.

quizQuestions.question: Time dilation means that a moving clock runs:
options: At the same rate as a stationary clock
Backward relative to a stationary clock
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Slower than a stationary clock.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "According to special relativity, the speed of light in a vacuum is:" is: Constant for all observers regardless of motion.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Constant for all observers regardless of motion is correct.
